questions.question: A pendulum swings back and forth with its height above the ground modeled by h(t) = 60 + 40cos(πt/2), where h is in centimeters and t is in seconds. What is the maximum height of the pendulum?
questions.explanation: The function h(t) = 60 + 40cos(πt/2) has a maximum when cos(πt/2) = 1. Thus max height = 60 + 40(1) = 100 cm.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side a = 7, side b = 9, and angle C = 60°. Using the Law of Cosines, find the length of side c. Round to the nearest tenth.
The graph of y = sin(2x) completes one full cycle over what interval on the x-axis?
questions.answer: [0, π] or any interval of length π
questions.explanation: For y = sin(2x), the period is 2π/2 = π. One complete cycle occurs over an interval of length π, such as from x = 0 to x = π.

questions.question: In triangle DEF, angle D = 40°, angle E = 75°, and side e = 12. Using the Law of Sines, find the length of side d. Round to the nearest tenth.
The function f(x) = 3sin(x − π/3) is shifted how many units to the right compared to y = sin(x)?
questions.explanation: The term (x − π/3) inside the sine indicates a horizontal shift of π/3 to the right. The amplitude 3 only stretches vertically, not horizontally.

questions.question: A triangular plot of land has sides of length 10 m, 14 m, and 18 m. Using the Law of Cosines, find the measure of the angle opposite the 18 m side. Round to the nearest degree.
questions.explanation: Let c = 18, a = 10, b = 14. By Law of Cosines: c² = a² + b² − 2ab·cos(C), so 324 = 100 + 196 − 280cos(C). Thus cos(C) = −28/280 = −0.1, giving C ≈ arccos(−0.1) ≈ 98°.
